hero.title: Knowledge, memory and citation-backed RAG behind one API
hero.sub: An HTTP server that stores documents, decisions, and your agents' long-term memory, then answers questions over them — every result filtered by the caller's permissions and backed by citations. It has no UI of its own: NullClaw agents and the NullHub and NullDesk apps consume it over plain HTTP, with records stored in SQLite by default or in Postgres.

alts.label: Build from source (Zig 0.16.0)
alts.code:
zig build
zig build test
zig build run -- --db .nullpantry/nullpantry.db

quickstart:
Build, run, and ask the server to describe itself:

```bash
# default local NullClaw-compatible profile
zig build

# run with local SQLite records (vendored, no system dependency)
# server listens on http://127.0.0.1:8765 by default
zig build run -- --db .nullpantry/nullpantry.db

# full runtime flag list
zig build run -- --help

# the running service publishes its own API manifest
curl -sS http://127.0.0.1:8765/v1/openapi.json
```

features.title: One retrieval path
features.body: Search (/v1/search), question answering (/v1/ask), and context packs — pre-assembled bundles of relevant material for an agent's prompt (/v1/context-packs) — all run one pipeline: filter by the caller's permissions, gather keyword and vector matches, expand through the knowledge graph, rerank, and return results with citations.

features.title: Permission-aware by invariant
features.body: An answer is only valid if every source, atom, relation, chunk and citation is visible to the requesting actor. Retrieval never leaks across scopes.

features.title: Backends chosen at compile time
features.body: Build flags decide what the binary contains. Records go to SQLite or Postgres; agent memory to Redis, ClickHouse, Markdown files, or hosted services like Mem0 and Zep; vectors to pgvector, Qdrant, LanceDB, Weaviate, Chroma, or OpenSearch. Pick a profile — nullclaw, minimal, full, or custom — and unused adapters are never compiled in.

features.title: NullClaw-compatible memory API
features.body: Point a NullClaw agent's memory backend at NullPantry and it works unchanged. The /v1/agent, /v1/memory, and /v1/agent-sessions routes cover create/read/update/delete, search, session history, and the feed/checkpoint/apply flow agents use to sync shared memory.

features.title: Works fully offline
features.body: Without provider config it uses deterministic local embeddings and extractive, citation-backed answers. SQLite is vendored; no external service is required.

features.title: Knowledge with a lifecycle
features.body: Proposed, verified, accepted, stale, deprecated, superseded — plus freshness, expiry and conflict detection. Decisions are superseded, not deleted.

note: Early: one tagged release (v2026.06.09) and no license file yet. Pre-1.0: config and CLI may change between releases.

plain.what: A single-binary HTTP service, written in Zig, that stores knowledge records (docs, specs, decisions, notes) and agents' long-term memory, and serves search, Q&A, and memory APIs on port 8765 by default.
plain.why: It exists so shared knowledge and agent memory live in one governed place instead of scattered per-agent files — with permissions enforced on every read and answers that always cite their sources.
plain.when: Reach for it when more than one agent or app needs the same searchable docs and shared memory — say, several NullClaw agents that should remember the same things — and you'd rather point them all at one URL than sync files between them.
